## 2026-04-18 13:10 — US-054
- Applied migration `us_054_list_my_campaigns_rpc` creating `public.list_my_campaigns()` — SECURITY DEFINER SQL function, `search_path = ''`, schema-qualified, returns the caller's listings plus per-row `applications_count` + `submissions_count` sub-selects. Ownership gate: `where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()`. Ordered `updated_at desc, created_at desc` to surface recently-edited campaigns first. Revoked from public/anon; granted EXECUTE to authenticated. Mirrors Pattern #118 (`list_my_applications` skeleton) exactly.
- Extended `src/components/primitives/CampaignCard.tsx` with two optional props: `meta?: readonly CampaignCardMetaItem[]` (renders a caption-style line under the footer, items joined by ` · `) and `statusLabel?: string` (passthrough to `StatusPill`'s existing label override so "active" listing_status can render the approved-green pill but labeled "Active" not "Approved"). Both default to undefined; existing callsites (Discover feed) unchanged. Added a defensive `?? currency` fallback inside `formatPrice` so an unknown currency code from an untyped RPC `text` cast degrades to the raw code rather than `undefined5` — reviewer-flagged mitigation for the `row.currency as CampaignCardCurrency` cast site at the screen layer.
- Replaced the `TabPlaceholder` body of `app/(lister)/campaigns.tsx` with the real screen: `supabase.rpc('list_my_campaigns')` → bucket rows by status into `active` (status='active') vs `inactive` (draft|paused|closed|archived) via a lookup table over all 5 enum values → render `<CampaignCard>` per row with `meta=[{applications, N}, {submissions, N}]` and `status`+`statusLabel` from a listing_status→StatusPill palette map. Segment labels include inline counts (ACTIVE (n) / INACTIVE (n)) — new affordance vs. Applications tab since the counts are cheap and the lister expects to see them at a glance. FAB routes to `/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1` mirroring Dashboard (docs/design.md §3.2). Loading → two SkeletonCards; error → red text; empty → "No active campaigns. Post your first bounty." / "No inactive campaigns." Cards rendered non-interactive (no onPress) — lister-side listing detail doesn't exist yet and AC doesn't require navigation; CampaignCard's `!interactive` guard handles this cleanly.
- Files changed:
  - `supabase/migrations/20260418070000_us_054_list_my_campaigns_rpc.sql` (new)
  - `src/types/supabase.ts` (regenerated — adds `list_my_campaigns` Function row)
  - `src/components/primitives/CampaignCard.tsx` (+ meta prop, + statusLabel passthrough, + formatPrice currency fallback)
  - `app/(lister)/campaigns.tsx` (placeholder → full screen)
  - `scripts/ralph/prd.json` (US-054 passes: true)
  - `scripts/ralph/progress.txt` (this entry)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-054-active-segment.png` (new — 1 active listing "Summer drop hero creators" with ACTIVE pill + "0 applications · 0 submissions" meta row)
  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-054-inactive-empty.png` (new — INACTIVE segment empty state)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events`, unrelated per Pattern #50) / mobile-mcp ✓ (tapped INACTIVE segment, confirmed segment switch + empty state, then tapped ACTIVE segment back, confirmed card + pill + meta all render correctly — acme_studio seed test transactional probe earlier confirmed cross-tenant isolation: 2 listings for the owner, 0 for another user)
- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit. One medium-confidence finding — the `row.currency as CampaignCardCurrency` cast in the screen is unsafe because the RPC returns `text` (not a typed enum), so an unknown currency would render `undefined5` via `CURRENCY_SYMBOL[currency]` lookup. Fixed defensively at the primitive layer by adding `?? currency` inside `formatPrice` so unknown codes degrade to the raw three-letter code. Two other points in the review were explicitly marked "verified correct" (RPC ownership gate, all-enum-values covered, non-interactive card safe) or "false lead on further inspection" (onRefresh error handling was actually correct).
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** When a primitive's typed enum prop (e.g. `CampaignCardCurrency = 'USD'|'EUR'|'GBP'`) is fed from a Postgres `text` column via an RPC, the screen-layer cast is unchecked. Don't rely on callsite discipline — add a fallback **inside the primitive's formatter** (`CURRENCY_SYMBOL[x] ?? x`) so unknown values degrade gracefully to the raw code instead of rendering `undefined<suffix>`. Same principle for any enum→symbol map that sits downstream of an untyped input.
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** StatusPill's existing `label?: string` override + the `statusLabel` passthrough on CampaignCard is the canonical way to reuse the 4-color semantic palette (pending/approved/rejected/cancelled) for **different** enums (listing_status, submission_status, etc.) without fragmenting the palette. Map the upstream enum → pill color + display label in a single `{pill, label}` lookup table at the screen layer; pass both to the primitive. Cheaper than introducing new pill variants for every enum in the DB.
  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** For `list_my_<things>` RPCs that aggregate counts from related tables (applications → listings, submissions → applications), prefer correlated subqueries in the SELECT list over `LEFT JOIN GROUP BY`. They're easier to reason about (one `select count(*) ... where x = outer.id` per count), and PG 17's planner handles them efficiently. The `list_my_campaigns` function does two subqueries for applications + submissions counts and stays readable at ~20 lines.
  - Reviewer subagent reported 3 "important" issues but two were false leads on further inspection — learning: spot-check the medium-confidence findings against the actual code rather than blindly applying. The one real finding (the currency cast) was worth the 4-line fix in `formatPrice`.
- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (above) Defensive fallback inside primitive formatters for enum-typed props fed by untyped RPC columns; StatusPill label override as the canonical palette-reuse pattern for non-matching enums; correlated subqueries over GROUP BY for count-aggregate list-my RPCs.

- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (above) Defensive fallback inside primitive formatters for enum-typed props fed by untyped RPC columns; StatusPill label override as the canonical palette-reuse pattern for non-matching enums; correlated subqueries over GROUP BY for count-aggregate list-my RPCs.
---

lines: - **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (above) Validator column-type parity (int32 cap on threshold); decouple post-success cleanup from success signal; DevRolePicker as the canonical dev JWT path.

+- Applied migration `us_054_list_my_campaigns_rpc` creating `public.list_my_campaigns()` — SECURITY DEFINER SQL function, `search_path = ''`, schema-qualified, returns the caller's listings plus per-row `applications_count` + `submissions_count` sub-selects. Ownership gate: `where l.lister_id = public.current_user_id()`. Ordered `updated_at desc, created_at desc` to surface recently-edited campaigns first. Revoked from public/anon; granted EXECUTE to authenticated. Mirrors Pattern #118 (`list_my_applications` skeleton) exactly.

+- Extended `src/components/primitives/CampaignCard.tsx` with two optional props: `meta?: readonly CampaignCardMetaItem[]` (renders a caption-style line under the footer, items joined by ` · `) and `statusLabel?: string` (passthrough to `StatusPill`'s existing label override so "active" listing_status can render the approved-green pill but labeled "Active" not "Approved"). Both default to undefined; existing callsites (Discover feed) unchanged. Added a defensive `?? currency` fallback inside `formatPrice` so an unknown currency code from an untyped RPC `text` cast degrades to the raw code rather than `undefined5` — reviewer-flagged mitigation for the `row.currency as CampaignCardCurrency` cast site at the screen layer.

+- Replaced the `TabPlaceholder` body of `app/(lister)/campaigns.tsx` with the real screen: `supabase.rpc('list_my_campaigns')` → bucket rows by status into `active` (status='active') vs `inactive` (draft|paused|closed|archived) via a lookup table over all 5 enum values → render `<CampaignCard>` per row with `meta=[{applications, N}, {submissions, N}]` and `status`+`statusLabel` from a listing_status→StatusPill palette map. Segment labels include inline counts (ACTIVE (n) / INACTIVE (n)) — new affordance vs. Applications tab since the counts are cheap and the lister expects to see them at a glance. FAB routes to `/(lister)/campaigns/new/step-1` mirroring Dashboard (docs/design.md §3.2). Loading → two SkeletonCards; error → red text; empty → "No active campaigns. Post your first bounty." / "No inactive campaigns." Cards rendered non-interactive (no onPress) — lister-side listing detail doesn't exist yet and AC doesn't require navigation; CampaignCard's `!interactive` guard handles this cleanly.
+  - `src/components/primitives/CampaignCard.tsx` (+ meta prop, + statusLabel passthrough, + formatPrice currency fallback)
+  - `scripts/ralph/screenshots/us-054-active-segment.png` (new — 1 active listing "Summer drop hero creators" with ACTIVE pill + "0 applications · 0 submissions" meta row)

+- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ (only pre-existing INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` on `public.events`, unrelated per Pattern #50) / mobile-mcp ✓ (tapped INACTIVE segment, confirmed segment switch + empty state, then tapped ACTIVE segment back, confirmed card + pill + meta all render correctly — acme_studio seed test transactional probe earlier confirmed cross-tenant isolation: 2 listings for the owner, 0 for another user)

+- Code-reviewer verdict: issues fixed in this commit. One medium-confidence finding — the `row.currency as CampaignCardCurrency` cast in the screen is unsafe because the RPC returns `text` (not a typed enum), so an unknown currency would render `undefined5` via `CURRENCY_SYMBOL[currency]` lookup. Fixed defensively at the primitive layer by adding `?? currency` inside `formatPrice` so unknown codes degrade to the raw three-letter code. Two other points in the review were explicitly marked "verified correct" (RPC ownership gate, all-enum-values covered, non-interactive card safe) or "false lead on further inspection" (onRefresh error handling was actually correct).

+  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** When a primitive's typed enum prop (e.g. `CampaignCardCurrency = 'USD'|'EUR'|'GBP'`) is fed from a Postgres `text` column via an RPC, the screen-layer cast is unchecked. Don't rely on callsite discipline — add a fallback **inside the primitive's formatter** (`CURRENCY_SYMBOL[x] ?? x`) so unknown values degrade gracefully to the raw code instead of rendering `undefined<suffix>`. Same principle for any enum→symbol map that sits downstream of an untyped input.

+  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** StatusPill's existing `label?: string` override + the `statusLabel` passthrough on CampaignCard is the canonical way to reuse the 4-color semantic palette (pending/approved/rejected/cancelled) for **different** enums (listing_status, submission_status, etc.) without fragmenting the palette. Map the upstream enum → pill color + display label in a single `{pill, label}` lookup table at the screen layer; pass both to the primitive. Cheaper than introducing new pill variants for every enum in the DB.

+  - **Promote to Codebase Patterns:** For `list_my_<things>` RPCs that aggregate counts from related tables (applications → listings, submissions → applications), prefer correlated subqueries in the SELECT list over `LEFT JOIN GROUP BY`. They're easier to reason about (one `select count(*) ... where x = outer.id` per count), and PG 17's planner handles them efficiently. The `list_my_campaigns` function does two subqueries for applications + submissions counts and stays readable at ~20 lines.

+  - Reviewer subagent reported 3 "important" issues but two were false leads on further inspection — learning: spot-check the medium-confidence findings against the actual code rather than blindly applying. The one real finding (the currency cast) was worth the 4-line fix in `formatPrice`.

+- **Promoting to Codebase Patterns:** (above) Defensive fallback inside primitive formatters for enum-typed props fed by untyped RPC columns; StatusPill label override as the canonical palette-reuse pattern for non-matching enums; correlated subqueries over GROUP BY for count-aggregate list-my RPCs.
